question: A client sends PUT /users/42 with only the email field in the request body. The server returns 200 OK, but the user's name and phone fields are now null. What caused this, and how should the API design be corrected?
answer: PUT replaces the entire resource at the given URL. Sending only {email} overwrites every field — name and phone become null because PUT does not merge. PATCH merges only the provided fields into the existing resource. PUT is idempotent (same request N times = same state), making retries safe. PATCH is not inherently idempotent. The fix: send the full resource on PUT, or use PATCH with JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7396) for partial updates.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  A["Client
PUT /users/42
.json {email: "a@b.com"}"] --> B["Server replaces
entire resource"]
  B --> C["Result stored:
{email: "a@b.com"}
name=null, phone=null
❌ Silent data loss"]

  D["Client
PATCH /users/42
.json {email: "a@b.com"}"] --> E["Server merges
changed fields only"]
  E --> F["Result stored:
{name: "Alice",
email: "a@b.com",
phone: "555-0100"}
✅ All fields preserved"]
